categories.description: A monitoring alert fired. P1 pages the primary on-call via PagerDuty/Opsgenie. P2 alerts primary on-call channel. P3 + P4 are dashboard-only (no push).

A new monitoring incident was opened. Includes grouped alert count + affected services + severity. P1 pages backup on-call in addition to primary. Auto-fires MonitoringIncidentEscalatedNotification after the SLA elapses.

A monitoring incident has been open past its SLA without acknowledgment. Pages the backup on-call per oncall_page_config.escalation_policy. P1 SLA = 5min; P2 = 15min; P3 = 60min. Every escalation increments the incident's escalation_count.

A health check has been unhealthy for 7+ consecutive runs but has not yet crossed an alert threshold. Leading indicator — the tenant admin should investigate before the check drives an alert. Includes check name, target, last error, consecutive failure count.

A monitoring notification provider (Sentry/Datadog/PagerDuty/…) has been marked unreachable after N consecutive delivery failures. Every fire is a P1 signal — the tenant's monitoring is telling them their monitoring is broken. Fires the failover routing (if PagerDuty: fail over to Opsgenie for pending notifications; if Opsgenie: fail over to PagerDuty).
A P1 incident resolved. Postmortem doc is required per the tenant's incident-response policy. Auto-creates a postmortem template + assigns to the incident's acknowledger. Due within 5 business days.
Opt-in weekly summary of tenant SLO health: alert counts by severity, incident counts, MTTR, top 5 firing policies, top 5 unhealthy checks. Every Monday 09:00 tenant-local.
